header.title: Mangrove replanting raises juvenile fish counts

gold.name: Instituto Aguas Claras
claims: Instituto Aguas Claras led a five-year study that found mangrove replanting along the Camorim estuary raised juvenile fish counts by 62 percent.
Instituto Aguas Claras published the Camorim estuary study in March 2026.
The Instituto Aguas Claras study of the Camorim estuary tracked 18 restored plots.
Instituto Aguas Claras is based in Recife.
Instituto Aguas Claras has monitored the Camorim estuary since 2012.
Instituto Aguas Claras plans to extend the estuary survey to two neighboring estuaries in 2027.
